entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum (cv. Maldandi) seeds used in the study

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pearl millet (cv. Sadoré local) seeds used in the study

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The water content of the seeds, adjusted to 6%, 10%, and 14%

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of seeds to sprout, measured as a percentage of normal seedlings

entities.name: VACUUM PACKING
entities.description: The process of removing air from aluminium foil envelopes to a pressure of -0.95 bar

entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which seeds remain viable during storage

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Storage temperatures used to accelerate ageing, specifically 50°C and 35°C

entities.name: VIABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of seeds to germinate and grow

entities.name: GERMINATION TEST
entities.description: Replicated tests on fifty seeds to estimate initial and subsequent viability

entities.name: HALF-LIFE
entities.description: The time taken for the viability of seeds to reduce to half of its initial value

entities.name: SEED SURVIVAL
entities.description: The ability of seeds to remain alive and viable under specific storage conditions

relationships.description: enhanced seed longevity at 6% moisture content
lower moisture content (6%) retained viability longer
significant effect on seed survival at 50°C
significant effect on seed survival at 50°C
survived longer than sorghum under similar conditions
survived shorter than pearl millet under similar conditions

estimates of half-life used to analyze seed survival
